title: sagina ciliata var. filicaulis
intro: Sagina ciliata var. filicaulis, commonly known as marsh dewgrass or water stitchwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Sagina ciliata var. filicaulis include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
